title: Space Rocks: Asteroid Samples Return to Earth
story: Zoom! A space capsule streaked across the sky, landing safely in Australia. It was Hayabusa-2, returning from an incredible journey. This Japanese spacecraft had visited an asteroid called Ryugu, scooped up rock samples, and brought them back to Earth. On December 6, 2020, scientists eagerly collected these precious space rocks - the first significant samples ever returned from an asteroid!

story: Astronomers made a jaw-dropping discovery in 2017! They spotted a colossal black hole lurking in the far reaches of space. title: London's Taxi Revolution
story: Hailing a cab became official business in London. On December 6, 1897, the city introduced the world's first licensed taxicabs. These horse-drawn carriages, called 'hackney carriages,' had to meet strict standards. Drivers needed special permits to operate. This system made rides safer and more reliable for Londoners and visitors alike.
activity_question: What were the first licensed taxicabs in London called?
bonus_fact: The word 'taxi' comes from 'taximeter,' a device that calculates the fare based on distance traveled.

title: Feathered Fortune: Audubon's Book Breaks Records

follow_up_question: Why do you think the pope chose a peddler as his disguise?

title: From Idlewild to JFK: An Airport's Birth
story: Planes soar over Queens today, but in 1941, it was just a dream. On December 6 that year, New York City Council gave the green light to build Idlewild Airport. This decision launched what would become one of the world's busiest travel hubs. Years later, Idlewild got a new name: John F. Kennedy International Airport, or JFK for short.
activity_question: What was the original name of the airport before it became JFK?
follow_up_question: Why do you think airports are important for a big city like New York?

title: Cavaliers Triumph in Thrilling Home Debut
story: Cheers erupted as the Cleveland Cavaliers clinched their first NBA home victory in 1970. On December 6, fans packed the arena to witness history. The Cavaliers faced off against the Buffalo Braves in a nail-biting showdown. With hearts racing, the home team emerged victorious, edging out their opponents 108-106. It was a moment that ignited basketball passion in Cleveland.
activity_question: Who did the Cleveland Cavaliers defeat in their first NBA home victory?
follow_up_question: How do you think the fans reacted when the Cavaliers won their first home game?
